title: Chest pain with conflicting risk history and escalation threshold

transcript.speaker: Clinician
transcript.text: Tell me about the chest pain.

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: It was a heavy pressure for about 20 minutes while walking up a hill. It settled when I rested.

transcript.speaker: Clinician
transcript.text: Any pain now, shortness of breath, sweating, nausea, or radiation?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: No pain now. I felt sweaty and a bit sick. It went to my left arm.

transcript.speaker: Clinician
transcript.text: Do you have diabetes, high blood pressure, smoking, or family history?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: I have high blood pressure and my father had a heart attack at 52. I don't smoke and I don't have diabetes.

transcript.speaker: Clinician
transcript.text: Your ECG here is not showing ST elevation, but this history is concerning. You need ambulance transfer to ED for troponins and monitoring. Take aspirin now unless allergic.

candidate_notes.note: Exertional chest pressure lasting 20 min, resolved with rest. Associated diaphoresis, nausea, left arm radiation. Risk factors: hypertension and father MI age 52. Denies smoking and diabetes. ECG no ST elevation in clinic. Impression possible ACS despite pain-free state. Aspirin if no allergy. Ambulance transfer to ED for serial troponins and monitoring.
Chest pain resolved. ECG reassuring. Patient has no diabetes and does not smoke. Likely exertional musculoskeletal pain. Advised GP follow-up and return if pain recurs.

S: 20 min heavy chest pressure on hill, resolved with rest. Sweaty, nauseated, left arm radiation. PMH hypertension. Father MI at 52. No smoking or diabetes. O: ECG without ST elevation. A: Concerning exertional chest pain, possible ACS. P: Aspirin if not allergic; transfer to ED for troponins and monitoring.

Possible ACS. Key positives: exertional heavy pressure, diaphoresis, nausea, left arm radiation, HTN, premature family history. Key negatives: pain-free now, no diabetes, non-smoker. ECG no ST elevation does not exclude ACS. Ambulance transfer to ED for troponins/monitoring; aspirin if no allergy.

CDSS chest pain pathway: high-risk history despite non-diagnostic ECG. Exertional pressure x20 min, left arm radiation, diaphoresis/nausea. HTN and family history of early MI. No diabetes/smoking. Action: aspirin if no allergy and ambulance transfer to ED for serial troponin, repeat ECG, cardiac monitoring.
